description: Throughout my career, I have enjoyed and excelled at empowering my clients to live better and more meaningful lives. I guide my clients to focus on their inner growth and self development, which in turn creates more purpose and balance in their lives. Let me empower and support you through your wellness and healing journey. Having a great therapist to guide you through transitions and stressors can take your goals to the next level. I'm here to help you to flourish in life. I specialize in working with adults and couples. I am able to help with individual mental health issues, relationship/marital conflict, addiction, and life transitions/stressors, to name a few. I enjoy incorporating CBT and Mindfulness techniques into treatment, but I use a variety of therapeutic modalities, tailored to what meets your needs best. I take a holistic and warm approach to therapy and am person-centered, empathic, and genuine in my practice. I am accepting new clients, and offering in-person therapy as well as Telehealth (remote video therapy). Christian counseling is available to clients upon request. Take the first step and begin your journey today!

Have you tried to get your young adult assistance only to feel frustrated? This can very challenging as providers are often booked and many campuses are short on staff.  

U Are Heard is the solution you have been looking for!  

We offer online therapy and coaching in an easy to use manner.  We work within your students schedule and bring help directly to your student the way they prefer (virtually)   We pride ourselves in getting students seen quickly and efficiently.  Now more than ever student are feeling anxious, so they are able to enjoy the experience of being a student to its fullest. Reach out and book a consultation today! Our all-star staff is made up of life coaches and clinicians all functioning as helpers to students.  We work within your students schedule, not the other way around. Our staff has a wide background of working with college aged students in a number of different areas.  We aim to meet the needs of all students that reach out so they are all HEARD. If you are the student and have reached out and have encountered obstacles I am sure you are frustrated. Or maybe the stigma of going to 'that floor' where the counselors are located at school seems too stigmatizing.  U Are Heard is the answer for you. We cut through all of that and bring the support directly to you. Book a consultation today!

Now accepting new clients for March 2026! 
A lot of people I work with describe feeling on edge all the time. They are worried and confused and their minds are always churning. They are functioning and often doing well. But the constant tension is getting to them and affecting their relationships too. They are tired of holding everything in every day and feeling like something is wrong, but it's hard to explain. They hope to be stronger and more independent and finally be able to face what they've been avoiding. And they just want to feel better and spend more time doing things that they enjoy. I’m a licensed clinical social worker helping people feel better since 2012. In our sessions, you can expect a warm, safe space to explore your thoughts, feelings, struggles and goals. Together, we’ll uncover the the strength inside of you, so you can experience that calm and move through life feeling more steady, energized, and confident. It takes courage for that first step toward healing. I’d love to connect and support you on this journey. Visit my website to set up a short phone call and explore if we can be a good fit.

At Wild Orchid Wellness, LLC (W.O.W), we are deeply committed to providing culturally competent and inclusive behavioral health services to individuals and families from all walks of life. Our team of compassionate and dedicated therapists are here to support your spiritual growth and mental well-being. We pride ourselves on being bilingual and bicultural, offering services in Spanish, Polish, and English to ensure everyone feels understood and valued (hablamos español, mówimy po polsku).  We are currently accepting new clients. Estamos aceptando clientes nuevos. ¡Llámenos hoy para una consulta gratuita de quince minutos! We have experience in working with substance use disorders, adult and childhood trauma, acute mental health, Intimate Partner Violence (IPV), and life transitions such as college, marriage, divorce, menopause, and becoming a new parent. Our practice is a safe space for the LGBTQ Plus Community providing affirmative therapy for greater self. Wild Orchid Wellness, LLC is a place where growth and lasting change happen. Learn how to break generational curses and cleanse your soul by doing the work. Expand your knowledge and create a safe space for spiritual growth.  If you are interested in embarking on a journey of growth and transformation, please reach out. Let's start your journey together.

Many people experience the weight of persistent thoughts, anxiety, depression, and a restless mind—and facing these struggles can feel isolating. With a warm and supportive approach, I’m here to walk alongside individuals as they work toward healing and clarity.  I am here to offer my services at Sage Valley Therapy, providing accessible and convenient virtual and in-person sessions. I work with individuals seeking support to better understand their emotions, improve their mood, and build healthier coping strategies. I integrate evidence-based approaches, including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Mindfulness, Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP), and culturally responsive trauma-focused care, to support clients in enhancing emotional regulation, reducing distress, and increasing a sense of control over their mental health. If you or a loved one is struggling with anxiety, depression, self-esteem, trauma, or OCD, please know that you are not alone. I warmly invite you to reach out to me. Together, we can navigate this journey towards wellness. Healing begins with a conversation, let's talk!

I am a licensed clinical social worker in Connecticut, Massachusetts and New York with over 25 years of experience helping preteens, adolescents, and adults, individually or as a couple or family, examine the current stressors that are getting in the way of life satisfaction. Having a safe place to express and explore these issues can be healing.  I believe that successful therapy comes very much from the connection of the therapist and client.  I will treat you, your feelings and experiences with respect and compassion. My style is dynamic and person centered and I integrate a variety of techniques for example CBT, DBT, solution focused treatments to help neurotypical and neurodivergent clients deal with anxiety, depression, chronic illness, life stage stress and relationship issues, while bringing a level of empathy to meet you where you’re at and make change from there. I look forward to working together toward your goals. Please reach out.

I am a devoted therapist that will provide competent services to adolescents, adults and couples seeking support to address and maintain emotional and physical well being. I believe in solution focused approach where clients are encouraged and supported to develop coping skills and strategies to find their own personal strengths to address stressors in everyday life. Forging a collaborative therapeutic relationship helps develop insight and understanding into old destructive behaviors and beliefs that get in the way of making positive change. I am dually licensed Clinical Social Worker and Licensed Alcohol and Drug counselor. I have extensive experience working with mood disorders, addiction, anxiety, parenting, behavioral concerns, divorce and relationship discord. I have a prescriber in my office should medication intervention be required.

If you are struggling with problems such as unresolved trauma, anxiety,  grief or depression, I will utilize the most appropriate technique to enable you to eliminate symptoms and achieve your goals.  I look forward to getting to know you, and making it possible for you to feel understood and less alone.  Perhaps you want  to feel better about yourself, have more fulfilling relationships, or improve the quality of your life.  Please contact me to discuss your needs and take a step towards a more meaningful, growth-oriented, healthy and satisfying life. I am certified in EMDR along with much training and practice of several other techniques such as cognitive therapy, modern analysis, relaxation and mindfulness meditation.   These methods can help you reduce anxiety and recover from any kind of traumatic or distressing experience.  They can also help with addictive behaviors, fears and all kinds of losses. Being able to say anything without being judged, and to be who you really are, is of paramount importance to me. It is my great pleasure and honor to facilitate self-actualization and symptom relief.  I value each person and appreciate every minute we get to spend focused on your healing and growth.

I am Angeles Ramos, a bilingual licensed marriage and family therapist experienced in working with children, adolescents, adults, families, and couples.

Do you feel frustrated or lost? Are you anxious, overwhelmed or depressed?  Do you feel unhappy, unmotivated, or stressed?   Are you worried and hurt seeing your child or teen experiencing emotional difficulties? You can help your child get the therapy support that can help make a difference in his or her life.  Are you suffering from past trauma or loss?  This is the time to reach out and begin your road to recovery and healing. Courage starts with showing up and letting ourselves be seen. -
Brene Brown. Therapy is the work of the heart. Going to therapy does not mean you are weak or flawed. To the contrary: Going to therapy means that you are ready to make changes, explore choices, and seek the support and guidance you need to work through the hurt that keeps you from being happy.  You are not alone. Let's begin today.

Hello! My name is Joseph, and I am a licensed therapist with a deep passion for helping individuals navigate life’s challenges, discover their potential, and foster meaningful growth. I understand that seeking therapy can feel like a big step, but I believe it’s a courageous one. No matter where you are in your journey, I am committed to walking alongside you as we explore solutions and new perspectives. My therapeutic approach is client-centered, meaning I tailor my methods to fit the unique needs and experiences of each individual. I combine elements of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), mindfulness, and solutions-focused approaches to help clients build self-awareness, develop healthy coping strategies, and overcome obstacles. If you're ready to take that next step toward healing and growth, I’d be honored to work with you. Please feel free to reach out to me for a consultation. I look forward to supporting you in your journey toward a healthier, more fulfilling life.

We all struggle. I am passionate about helping families and individuals. Relationships are the heartbeat of our lives, but they can also be our greatest source of struggle. I work collaboratively with individuals, couples, and families in a safe, nonjudgmental environment where nothing surprises me and everyone can be heard and understood. My approach is empathetic yet action-oriented; I believe that when you show up differently, the world around you shifts in response. My path wasn't linear. After a career in consulting, I spent years raising my children. That period was both challenging and fulfilling, and ignited my vow to advocate for families facing similar hurdles. Today, I combine my professional training in Marriage and Family Therapy with real-world lived experience to help clients redefine their place in the world Whether you are navigating your own personal growth and acceptance or one of life’s transitions, parenting hurdles, or a relationship that feels stuck, you don't have to do it alone. Together, we can begin the work of creating meaningful and sustainable change. Please reach out for a free consultation. I look forward to speaking with you.

I have been working with grief, both normal and complicated, for nearly 35 years and have specialized training in treating both. For those who are going through a normal albeit difficult time coping with the loss of a loved one, or if you are a caregiver for someone with a stressful illness (e.g., cancer, Alzheimer's), help is here. I also have extensive experience in treating the needs of the LGBTQ community. I strongly prefer to meet clients in person due to the type of interventions I utilize that are more effective this way. Grief is not only a reaction to death of a loved one. It can also mask itself within the loss of independence due to health and/or mental health challenges, or the changing of one's identity. While painful, grief and can also lead to positive change and newfound strength once you walk through it and can feel what's on the other side. Therapy is a serious commitment that is not to be entered into lightly. If you are ready to "do the work", I am ready to help. It's especially important that you take this step for yourself, not just because others may have suggested you do so. If you feel ready to tolerate the ups and downs that good therapy provides, the hard work is truly worth it.

In a world that often tells us to figure everything out on our own, asking for help is a true sign of strength. As a clinician, my goal is to create a safe, supportive, non-judgmental environment where we can work to create change. My approach is client-centered and solution-focused. Together, we will develop the tools to reach your goals. I utilize modalities of CBT, ACT, and Mindfulness. I have worked with children, adolescents, and adults in school, outpatient, intensive outpatient, and residential treatment settings. I am certified in Trauma-Focused Cognitive Behavioral Therapy. My passion for being a therapist stems from the belief that every person deserves to live a fulfilling life. I hope to guide you, empower you, and help you tap into your unique strengths. Feel free to reach out at any time to see if it might be a good fit. I look forward to helping you take the next step in your journey, and creating a life you love.

Searching for a therapist can feel overwhelming, but you don’t have to do it alone. With a warm and caring approach, I provide a supportive space where you can feel comfortable and understood. If you’re new to therapy, I’ll guide you through each step, and if you’re returning, we’ll build on your goals together. My approach is warm, flexible, and strength-based, helping clients feel comfortable, supported, and understood. I focus on highlighting each person’s unique strengths, resilience, and potential while creating a compassionate space that honors their individuality, values, and goals. I enjoy working with teens, young adults, and adults on anxiety, depression, emotional regulation, coping skills, communication, self-esteem, stress, overall well-being, friendships, and life transitions. Let’s take the next step together.

Growing up as a kid I needed a therapist. At this point In my life I don't remember what we went over, but I remember how I felt. Since then I've been determined to be the best therapist I can be. I'll work with you no matter what the issue, and I promise you that we are in this together. It won't be easy, but nothing good ever is. Session can be in person or virtual. They can even be asynchronous. We are creative in terms of how we provide therapy. It can be through text, video messages, email, phone call etc. The reason these services are offered is because I truly believe consistent daily contact is critical to your success. Give me a call! We won't know if we are a good fit for each other unless we have a conversation.

Hello, my name is Stephanie and I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker with over 13 years of experience. I am passionate about supporting individuals who are struggling with anxiety, depression, difficult life transitions, or a general sense of feeling stuck or unheard. I provide a nonjudgemental, safe and supportive space where you can openly discuss even the most uncomfortable topics. Our first couple of sessions together will have a more of a formal feel to them. I will ask you a lot of questions about yourself in order to assess how to best support you in the therapeutic process. I will be curious to know the reason you are seeking therapy at this time and what your expectations are of therapy. The feedback that I've received from past clients is that they feel comfortable with me. They felt supported, heard and validated. I also use humor when appropriate, which previous clients have appreciated. Clients have told me that they felt like I was "on their side" through the process.

You may find yourself people-pleasing, withdrawing, overfunctioning, or longing for closeness, unsure how to truly let someone in. You may be experiencing anxiety that never settles, pain that lingers, or relationship patterns that keep repeating, no matter how hard you try to change them. These struggles aren't failures, but ways you learned to survive when connection felt uncertain, needs went unmet, or emotions felt too big to experience alone. The strategies that once protected you, or got you the crumbs of what you needed, may now leave you feeling stuck, exhausted, and far from the closeness, compassion, and calm you long for. I can help you uncover the experiences and beliefs that drive your patterns. We lean into the parts that learned to stay small, strong, vigilant, or isolated, helping you reconnect with your needs, voice, and capacity for deeper connection. Healing means finally having the room, safety, and permission to be fully yourself and share it with those you love. I help couples slow down painful cycles and uncover the vulnerable emotions and unmet needs beneath. We'll organize the misses and protective reactions that keep you stuck, help you deepen emotional connection, and rebuild security. I'll help you show up more openly, respond to each other with care, and create emotional intimacy that both of you can rely on.

People come to therapy for a variety of reasons. You may be feeling stuck as an individual, have a challenge in your relationship, or are experiencing a difficult life transition.  I work with individuals, as well as couples, and families. I have an aptitude and training in working with families and individuals of the LGBTQ community. My approach is to create a compassionate environment where we can collaborate to identify the issues and patterns that are producing unhappiness in your life. My therapy space – virtual or in person – is a place where you can explore the issues that are creating barriers in your life and provide strategies and solutions to get you to a position to find peace My style is warm, pragmatic and non-judgmental, as I work with you to navigate through some of life transitions. My goal is to create a safe and trusting environment where, together, we can develop a roadmap to meet your needs and goals. I am located in Fairfield County, CT.

Making the decision to seek therapy is the start of your journey in finding the calmness within you. I will guide you towards exploring the challenges that prevent you from moving forward in achieving your personal growth and healing. My role as a therapist is to help you access those parts of yourself that are hurt, lost or confused. I am here to support, and guide you through what feels impossible to do alone. Sessions will include self reflection, and exploration to help you gain better insight into the underlying cause of your symptoms. I provide a supportive environment based on preserving and respecting my client's culture. My professional experience consists of treating children, adolescents, families and adults. I am passionate and devoted with providing my clients quality service. To name a few, my approach is eclectic applying person-centered, Rational Emotive behavior therapy, Trauma focus, strength base approach, cognitive behavioral, and Dialectical behavior therapy. A graduate from UCONN, and working over 25 years in a clinical behavioral health setting provided me with the knowledge and skill set to help others reach their goals. Taking the first step to ask for help can be difficult, finding your match is not easy, I been in that place. My personal and professional experience will help make the transition welcoming.

People have the unending capacity to heal, cope, and change their lives even through life’s most difficult challenges. I have a passion for helping individuals cope and find peace during challenging situations. I utilize an integrative therapeutic approach that is focused on each individual’s strengths and needs. I believe in a holistic approach that considers the mind-body interaction. I have had experience in neurofeedback and continue to learn about how it can be utilized with mental health counseling. I have experience working in intensive clinical settings. I worked with individuals from many identities, ability statuses, and walks of life to help increase their well-being and live valued lives. It would be an honor for me to join you on your journey to heal.

Life can be stressful as you are faced with numerous decisions and choices as you navigate the challenges of family, work, school and/or finances. As you face the complexities of life, you may be experiencing worries or other negative thoughts which can lead to feelings of fear, sadness and/or anger. I will support you in promoting emotional wellness by focusing on your strengths and helping you realize your potential to live a fulfilling life. Together we will explore treatment methods that can help you effectively navigate challenging times. I will empower you and support you along your journey. I am accepting new clients through MellaHealth. Our care coordinator will work with you to identify the best-fit therapist for your needs. Call, email, or schedule your consultation online to begin working with your therapist.

Managing negative thoughts can be a challenging journey, even for the strongest individuals. It’s essential to remember that negative thoughts can lead to addiction which is a disease of the brain, not a moral failing. You are not a bad or flawed person. Like other chronic diseases, addiction, anxiety and depression require ongoing care to manage. Recovery is a lifelong commitment that won’t always be easy, but it's possible because problems have solutions. I use a combination of therapeutic and supportive strategies such as motivational incentives and rewards, support networks, empathic listening, teaching how to accept change, behavior education in an outpatient treatment setting. Residential treatment and detox referral services available. Counseling can be an effective treatment strategy. It can provide support from a knowledgeable and understanding therapist. Therapy can offer support to show that there are other people who have struggled with similar problems and are overcoming them. An important step in treatment is learning about adaptive strategies to manage negative emotions.

Hello, I am a dually board-certified and credentialed Family Nurse Practitioner with over 25 years of experience in healthcare. Born and raised in New Haven County, I have dedicated my career to serving the community that shaped and molded me. I am a 2022 graduate of Simmons University’s Advanced Practice Registered Nurse program. I am governed by a strong foundation of evidence-based, patient-centered care that I impart into my treatment care model. 

During my off time, I am the proud fur mom to a beloved brood of rescue pups, where I find joy and balance in the unconditional love they bring. I have spent nearly a decade treating a wide range of mental health conditions. I am a passionate advocate for a comprehensive approach to care, recognizing the vital connection between mental, physical, and emotional well-being. My mission includes dispelling stigmas around behavioral health and helping build healthier futures for all I service. The road to success of any sort is built on trust, perseverance, and hard work. I look forward to helping you build your "toolbox" for success.

Through a warm and non-judgmental therapeutic environment, she provides a safe space for clients to explore their emotions, understand the underlying causes of their difficulties, and develop practical strategies to manage symptoms. She is passionate about empowering her clients to achieve personal growth and well-being. She collaborates closely with individuals to identify their strengths, build resilience, and enhance self-esteem. Through personalized treatment plans, she helps clients set and achieve realistic goals, fostering positive change. Comprehensive expertise in assessment, diagnosis, and treatment of mental health disorders. She tailors her therapeutic techniques to meet the unique needs of each client. She draws from evidence-based modalities, including CBT, DBT, and solution-focused therapy, to help individuals develop effective coping strategies and and achieve positive change.

specializations: Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACEs), Anxiety, Trauma and PTSD
description:
Do you ever feel like you’re doing your best to hold it all together, but underneath you’re exhausted; tired of being strong, tired of pretending, tired of carrying things no one else sees? Maybe you’ve had to grow up fast, care for others before learning to care for yourself, or survive in spaces that didn’t always make room for your voice. Those experiences can leave marks, shaping how you love, trust, and see yourself.
I help adolescents, young adults, and women work through trauma, anxiety, and depression, especially when those challenges are rooted in Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACEs) or difficult life transitions. My clients come from communities where strength is expected, vulnerability is misunderstood, and faith has been an anchor. I make space for your story, culture, faith, and healing. As a Licensed Clinical Social Worker, my trauma-informed, faith-centered approach honors both resilience and restoration. In our sessions, you can expect a calm, compassionate space where faith, identity, and healing can coexist without judgment. You deserve care that helps you feel whole, not just functional. Healing begins with one honest conversation, and that conversation can start here.

A wise friend once told me that getting involved in therapy is one of the greatest gifts that you can give yourself, and I think she was right.  I am a clinical social worker with over 20 years of experience working with adolescents, adults, and families experiencing some of life's tough times. I believe that these tough times can pass with some help and support. Using a variety of evidence-based practices, I help clients learn how to handle stressors, navigate through difficult situations, build resiliency, tap into their own strengths and heal. I find creative thinking, insight, patience, and humor to be essential for working through many issues. I am a friendly, down-to-earth person and see my role as more of a coach or support as we work towards goals together.

Welcome -
If you're considering starting therapy, you've tried other things and are still dissatisfied with your life.  You may find yourself worried, feeling down, having problems in your relationships....things can feel blocked.  Working together, we can find the opportunity for growth in these difficulties, and the way out of the maze.  Your life can come more into focus, you can realize what you really want, and go after it.  You didn't choose these difficulties or the difficult times we live in, but within the difficulties we can find a call to your future. My approach is collaborative and highly interactive.  Problems can have roots, involving life patterns that trouble you.  Dreams are a practical, useful tool for understanding what's driving you that you may not be aware of  - both today's troubles and your desires for the future.  This is referred to as depth psychology I invite you to a first conversation - let's see if it feels right for us to work together on this important undertaking.  Let's enter into dialogue.

I am currently accepting new clients for daytime scheduling.

I specialize in working with adults and emerging adults navigating anxiety, perfectionism, and relationship dynamics. My approach focuses on helping clients identify and break unhelpful patterns, improve communication in relationships, and manage the anxiety that change can often bring about. I take a collaborative approach to help recognize patterns that might be holding you back from putting yourself first and identify ways for you to prioritize your needs in a way that feels comfortable and sustainable. You are the expert on yourself. My goal is to help you make positive changes aligned with your values. If you are curious about exploring how to work towards putting yourself first and creating more balance, or simply want to talk through relational issues, I encourage you to reach out and see if it's a good fit.

You may be exhausted, disconnected, or feeling empty and looking for some hope. You may feel alone on this journey and believe something needs to change. Perhaps you are in a relationship that just doesn’t feel right or you have experienced something that has left you feeling afraid, out of control, or numb. Maybe you are navigating a difficult journey toward motherhood, grieving the loss of one or more pregnancies, or have arrived at motherhood to realize that this is not what you had expected. My respectful, empathetic, and empowering approach will help you in discovering strategies to increase your self-care, find balance, establish healthy boundaries to help you feel more connected to others, and understand and manage your symptoms. I am here to offer compassionate care. You are not alone, and with help, you will be well. Taking the first step toward getting help can feel intimidating. I strongly believe that a connection between the clinician and the client is essential for effective treatment, which is why I offer a free phone consultation to see if we could work well together. Change is possible. Let me help.

You may be feeling overwhelmed by anxiety, stress, or the weight of constant expectations. Perhaps you’re navigating a life transition, struggling with confidence, or finding that relationships—whether with family, partners, or yourself—feel more challenging than they should. You may want clarity, relief, or a sense of direction, but aren’t sure where to start or how to move forward. I work with children, adolescents, young adults, and families who are seeking support during these moments. My role is to help you better understand what you’re experiencing, identify your goals, and develop practical tools to manage challenges in a way that feels manageable and empowering. I offer a structured, goal-oriented, and supportive approach. Therapy with me is collaborative, nonjudgmental, and strengths-based. I believe you already have the capacity for growth, and my goal is to help you access and build upon that strength in a supportive environment. If you’re ready to take the next step toward feeling more confident, grounded, and supported, I invite you to reach out.

If you’re feeling overwhelmed, disconnected, or weighed down by stress or trauma, you’re not alone, and you don’t have to navigate it by yourself. I’m a trauma-trained, somatic, person-centered therapist who helps women, mothers, and trauma survivors reconnect with themselves, establish healthy boundaries, and find a sense of internal safety and clarity. My clients often tell me they feel more comfortable, grounded, and understood in therapy than they expected. Together, we move gently and intentionally, at a pace that supports your nervous system and honors your lived experience. You are supported in building self-trust. My therapeutic style is warm, attuned, collaborative, & grounded in the belief that healing happens when you feel safe, seen, and supported. This means we go at your pace, never rushed, never pushed. Your voice, choices, and experiences guide our work.
Your body’s wisdom is honored. You are supported in building emotional resilience and boundaries. Sessions with me are collaborative, present, and paced with intention. You won’t be talked over or told what to feel. We’ll explore what’s coming up for you with curiosity, compassion, and steady guidance. Together we can make sense of your experiences and create a life you love, not just one you tolerate. Reach our today for a no obligation consultation.

Hello! I am a skilled and empathic clinician who assists with parenting strategies and attachment concerns due to possible trauma. I believe it is extremely important to have consistent emotional and therapeutic support when faced with difficulties in your life.   We will create and work towards your goals together. I want individuals to feel like our sessions are a safe place to talk freely. I also offer clinical supervision to clinicians who feel they may need both therapeutic and emotional support. I am trained in Child Parent Psychotherapy (CPP), Trauma Systems Therapy (TST) and various play therapy techniques. I have over 25 years of clinical experience working with young children and families. I have engaged in Parent Training sessions with parents of very young children with special needs. I also have worked with many individuals that have experienced trauma. I am looking forward to to supporting you and joining you in your therapy journey. See you soon! Please feel free to reach out to me if you need a safe space to discuss issues and concerns.  Life can be overwhelming with the multiple roles individuals take on in their lives.  Parenting, maintaining personal relationships and a career can leave little time for yourself.  Our sessions can be a time for you to express yourself and feel heard and empowered.

Our Declaration of Independence states that we are entitled to “Life, Liberty and the Pursuit of Happiness.” Chesapeake Reflections’ will help each individual attain those goals on a personal basis.  In life, there are always challenges.  When we learn that challenges do not have to overwhelm us, we can begin to enjoy life and be free of anxiety, worry and despair about the things that we cannot change, but that are present in our lives.  As we do so, each individual can have a life of happiness and contentment. We wish each of our clients to be able to reflect to others both the talents and the inner beauty that each person possess My first experience In counseling was at Southern Connecticut State University where our class was the second in the USA to train specifically for "co-occurring disorders" of Mental Health and Substance Abuse.. Through advanced training , I have expanded my skill set to include  Trauma,  PTSD, Anxiety, Depression, Grief Management,  Male Depression, My mission is to help you find happiness and contentment in life. I utilize multiple approaches   selectively for each client's individual needs.   A few of these are Collaborative Therapy,   Positive Psychology, Motivational Interviewing, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Mindfulness, etc. This is planned early in treatment so as to attain a timely success.

Each of us have experienced a point in time where we have been overwhelmed by trauma, problems at home, work or in the community.  These problems may be issues including anxiety, depression, domestic violence, sexual assault, work place violence, death of a loved one, co-parenting, anger, divorce, work place stress or substance abuse.  These stressors may impact daily life and can become overwhelming and require the need for outside support from a trained, licensed professional in order to work through challenges. As a Licensed Clinical Social Worker, I provide a supportive, non-judgmental environment in which you can discuss your concerns.   Some specialties include: trauma, domestic violence, sexual assault, court-ordered therapy, relationship issues, adolescence, self-esteem, anxiety, depression, and anger issues.  Trained in EMDR. Taking the step to seek professional help is a commendable decision, and I congratulate you on this journey toward healing and growth. For a phone consultation, please call (860) 494-3800. I am excited about the opportunity to work with you and support you on your path to well-being. For more details, feel free to explore my website at revivectllc.com.

Practice is accepting new clients! Reach out for a free consultation. 
Many of the people who seek my support are successful, capable, and deeply resilient but privately carrying the weight of complex relationships, unresolved trauma, or years of emotional exhaustion.

I specialize in helping individuals and couples heal from narcissistic and emotionally abusive relationship dynamics, rebuild their sense of self, and develop healthier relational patterns. Many of my clients come to therapy feeling confused, depleted, or questioning their own reality after experiencing chronic gaslighting, manipulation, or relational trauma. As a Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist and founder of My Story Counseling, my work is rooted in understanding the deeper relational systems that shape how we connect, trust, and experience intimacy. 
My approach is direct, compassionate, and insight-oriented. I integrate evidence-based trauma therapies including EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization and Re For couples, I offer focused relationship intensives designed to accelerate progress in a structured, supportive environment. These extended sessions allow partners to move beyond surface-level conflict and begin addressing the deeper dynamics that often keep couples stuck. At this time, I primarily work with clients through specialized therapy intensives.

I am a Licensed Professional Counselor and Registered Art Therapist with seven years of clinical experience providing specialized support to children, adolescents, and young adults. She provides individualized, person-centered therapy tailored to each client’s needs, with expertise in depression, anxiety, and ADHD. I incorporates a variety of art techniques—such as drawing, painting, and multimedia—along with CBT skills to help clients explore emotions, build coping strategies, and promote personal growth. I primarily work with clients aged 10 and older but will consider working with younger children when appropriate.

Andrew approaches his work with clients drawing upon Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Acceptance and Commitment therapy (ACT) approaches, DBT skills, EMDR, Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP) interventions. For me, relationships in my therapeutic work stand to be the backbone and foundation of the best possible outcomes. I am a warm and compassionate clinician who balances empathy and expectations while I help you face your fears and broaden your life. I use a combination of strategies with a compassion focused therapeutic approach. Experience in a number of mental health, therapeutic, and educational settings, providing counseling, consultation, assessment and behavior management interventions for numerous social-emotional difficulties. I work with children, teens and  adults with a broad range of diagnoses. Initial consultation is free of charge. My goal is to help you replace unhelpful, rigid coping patterns with healthy, flexible, value focused social-emotional skills while promoting cognitive flexibility.

As a Licensed Counselor, my professional experience includes providing individual, group, and family therapy, crisis intervention and cognitive-behavioral therapy to diverse populations. I am willing and committed to helping you build healthy relationships, positive attachments, and problem-solving skills to enhance and develop your personal and professional outlook. I chose the counseling profession because I enjoy people. I enjoy listening, learning, and helping individuals process their own worldviews. The counseling relationship should empower everyone to work through their unique situations, then develop skills to manage and cope in healthy and more fulfilling ways, I will assist in this process. During each session, you will become more aware of the possibilities and benefits of change, versus the consequences of staying the same, and we will work toward this goal together. CBT, and solution focus therapy teaches the idea of change through positive thinking and behavior modification, and we will use these techniques to help you gain a better outlook

I have experience working with preteens, teens, young adults, and adults across the lifespan. I have a focus on mood disorders, anxiety, depression, substance use, and self harming behaviors. I have an extensive background in methadone and working specifically with individuals struggling with opioid use disorder.  I have worked in a wide range of settings such as hospitals, outpatient clinics, jails, crisis, and group homes. Working in these different settings has helped me to experience a wide range of challenges and issues that I have helped individuals to overcome. Reaching out for help is hard. Finding the right person that you click with can be even harder. My goal is to meet you where you are and help you to feel comfortable while discussing the uncomfortable.  I aim to be person centered, strengths based, empathetic, and trauma informed when working with you. I will hold a warm, professional, and safe space for you to be vulnerable. I will use my experiences and clinical training to help develop coping skills and decrease symptom severity.  I will also help you learn how to utilize these coping skills on a daily basis.  I look forward to helping you make positive, long lasting changes in your life!

Are you looking to find more balance in your life? Today’s world seems to be constantly buzzing with an overwhelming amount of information, making it hard to see through it all to know where we are going. Let me help guide you closer to your path and your goals with a greater sense of balance. I have over 15 years of experience in the mental health field, with specialized training in Dialectic Behavioral Therapy and Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, and a strong focus on integrating all parts of ourselves in the process. My goal is to hold a safe, welcoming space for you to take pause and find relief. Life often shows its gifts when we slow down and take notice.

Being raised in an urban environment, I saw firsthand how there were often cracks in the systems that are designed to help people struggling with their mental health. I wanted to make sure that people had access to quality mental health care, which led to me starting my career by working with individuals and families who had experienced trauma. I am also passionate about working with individuals who are neurodiverse, part of the LGBTQ+ community, and "geek" communities. I think one of my biggest strength is that I am collaborative. I may be an expert when it comes to therapy, but I am not an expert in you. I want to hear what you think feels helpful and also when things don't feel helpful. Part of the therapeutic journey is about making connections and being provided with the support you need to be autonomous and happy.

specializations: Antisocial Personality, Bipolar Disorder, Borderline Personality (BPD)
description: I am available to treat adults of any and all race, and religious ideology.  There is no ideal client per say.  I seek to work with individuals who are struggling and are looking to get a handle on their daily life and mental wellbeing.  I hope each client will bring a level of commitment to therapy and a desire to work on their stated goals through the use of coping skills, and different types of therapeutic interventions, which will be taught and discussed to allow the client to attain their goals. I am a LIcensed Clinical Social Worker with a diverse background in prior work and type of clients treated.  I am a  Certified Clinical Trauma Professional, trained in Harm Reduction, DBT, CBT, Trauma centered care and more.  I enjoy working with a variety of clients with diverse backgrounds. Feel free to contact me to discuss treatment options and to see if I would be a good fit for you.    I also have a number of Clinical Social Workers I work with when another resource is needed.  In addition I offer clinical supervision to colleagues at minimal to no cost.

Sam specializes in CBT and DBT – two of the most effective evidence-based therapeutic modalities – to help clients of all ages change unhelpful thought processes, face their fears, learn better emotional regulation, and build interpersonal skills. Sam’s solution-focused, client-centric treatment helps clients define their goals and allows them to leverage their own strengths and support systems to achieve them. Clients praise his warm demeanor, which makes them feel heard and valued during sessions, and leave treatment with newfound confidence and the tools to navigate life's challenges successfully and lead more fulfilling lives. Sam helps his clients navigate life transitions, improve self-esteem, deepen relationships, process difficult events, and achieve personal growth – whatever the challenges they face. His decade of experience in schools and foster care make him uniquely equipped to support children and adolescents, including those with behavioral problems. Sam is committed to offering the most welcoming, approachable care possible. He provides in-person and virtual therapy with convenient afternoon and evening availability. Outside of his psychotherapy practice, Sam’s role as a school athletics coach provides him with valuable insights into the needs of athletes.

Hello, my name is Nickisha. I provide trauma-informed integrative treatment with individualized techniques based on evidence-based practices. I favor an eclectic approach to therapy, recognizing that each person is unique with distinct needs. My experience encompasses working with a diverse range of individuals, families, and groups, offering treatment for mental health, trauma, and substance use disorders. I am dedicated to collaborating on this healing journey and ensuring an empathetic, respectful, and therapeutic environment.

At SMP we support children, teens, and adults who may be experiencing social, communication, sensory, emotional, or developmental challenges. Parents may worry about milestones, behavior, or school difficulties. Adults may seek clarity about lifelong struggles or a possible autism diagnosis. Clients want an accurate evaluation, respectful care, and clear recommendations in a supportive environment. We offer comprehensive Autism Spectrum Disorder evaluations using the ADOS-2, clinical interviews, questionnaires, and behavioral observation. Our team helps clarify diagnoses, rule out other conditions, and guide clients toward appropriate supports and services. Wondering if autism is part of the picture can feel overwhelming. You don’t have to navigate this alone. We offer a thoughtful, compassionate evaluation process and invite you to reach out when you’re ready for answers.

You don’t want to be in therapy forever, but something has to change. You feel anxious, depressed, and burnt out. You’re a pro at keeping it together, showing up for everyone else, and getting it all done, but lately it’s getting harder. You want to get back to feeling like your old self, but you’re not entirely sure where to start.
New stages in life, complicated relationships, and impostor syndrome can make you feel overwhelmed, unmotivated and stuck. If these things are affecting your sleep, causing you to isolate from friends, or making it hard to enjoy anything, it might be time to try therapy. Whatever the changes you want therapy can help you identify what is making you feel stuck, so you feel less overwhelmed, more in control, and better able to enjoy your life.
I have 25+ years of experience helping people with depression, anxiety, parenting issues, relationship issues, impostor syndrome, life transitions, and gender identity issues. Therapy is a place to talk to someone objective, someone who doesn’t launch into their own story or experience. Someone who is just there for YOU, and and it helps if that someone has been doing this for a while. Therapists have different strengths and styles, and you need the right fit. If I sound like I might be a good fit for you, let’s talk!

I’m Allissa Tamiso, a Licensed Professional Counselor in Connecticut. If you're feeling stuck, overwhelmed or uncertain, let’s start unpacking your unique story to see how you got here. I specialize in helping individuals who are facing challenges with PTSD, ADHD, autism, anxiety, attachment issues, self-esteem, and life transitions. My approach is personalized to meet your individual needs by creating a safe, collaborative, and non-judgmental space for self-reflection and growth. I’m here to help you build emotional resilience and deepen your self-knowledge. In our work together, I’ll help you develop curiosity toward your emotional patterns and beliefs that influence your behaviors, fostering greater self-awareness and lasting change. I’m passionate about incorporating Self-compassion practices, inviting you to treat yourself with kindness (and even humor) during difficult moments. I also use Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) to teach practical skills for managing emotions, improving relationships, and cultivating mindfulness. I believe that self-compassion and insight are essential for healing, and my goal is to support you in living an authentic and meaningful life. I look forward to meeting you!

I have successfully worked with adults and children, couples and families, with both common and complex needs. My approach focuses on experiential awareness and processing, while working through present day thoughts, feelings, and behaviors. It is holistic in that it views symptoms as attempts at coping.We work together establishing a calm place within you, through the enhancement of personal awareness, resources, and developing coping strategies; we then progress beyond coping skills, moving through the roots of old learning so that your change is lasting, and from the inside out. My experience includes working in both a psychiatric hospital for 27 years and local community mental health agencies.
My training has moved me in the direction of mind-body psychotherapy approaches. I am family-systems oriented in thinking, and I tend to focus on helping clients with emotion regulation issues in practice. In addition to Marriage and Family therapy, I am trained in EMDR , IFS , and DBT; these approaches work well with various issues including anxiety, trauma/stress, impulsive behaviors, & mood disorders. Also EMDR has been successful with enhancing workplace performance, aiding in the reduction of performance anxiety, competition preparation, and psychological recovery from injury for athletes.

Welcome! Are you struggling with anxiety, disordered eating, body image concerns, life stressors, or relationship challenges? You don’t have to navigate it alone. I specialize in working with teens and adults experiencing eating disorders, binge eating, restrictive eating, emotional eating, anxiety, depression, and relationship stress. Many of my clients struggle with perfectionism, overthinking, and difficulty setting boundaries. Together, we focus on reducing anxiety, improving your relationship with food and your body, and building healthier coping strategies that feel sustainable. I will take the time to get to know your individual needs and help "you" develop personal goals for treatment. I will help individuals build self esteem and strengths, and empower individuals to advocate and take control of one's life. Together, the unmanageable can feel more manageable. We will build upon strengths to meet goals and to maintain a healthy emotional lifestyle. I provide in person and Telehealth sessions. Let me be part of your healing journey and email me today! I have a red toy poodle hypoallergenic therapy dog currently in training who may be present during sessions.

Sometimes we all need a little encouragement, guidance, validation, motivation, and a safe space to voice our concerns, feelings, thoughts, ideas, and questions. I am willing to be that person for you!  I am Valerie Barrett- Noel, a licensed independent social worker in Connecticut, Massachusetts and Florida. I graduated with my master's in social work from Fordham University. I completed my undergrad in criminal justice investigative services at the University of New Haven with concentrations in fire science and victim services. What makes Self Reflections Therapy unique is the ability to help you better address your trauma. I currently specialize and I am certified in Trauma Recovery Empowerment Model. This is an evidence based model that focuses on healing from trauma. I can help with both therapy and emotional support through ongoing cases. Services are also person centered! I have worked with clientele from all different backgrounds, cultures, and ages in spectrums such as domestic violence victims/survivors, sexual assault, homicide, substance abuse, and integrating those who have been incarcerated back into the community for over 6 years.     My focus in therapy sessions is working together to address concerns holistically.

There are times in our lives when we don't know what is wrong; just that something is.  We wonder if we are "crazy" and are scared to be judged if we ask for help. The truth is that very sane people struggle when the difficulties in their lives outpace their coping skills. I've been a therapist for 18 years and believe strongly that healing comes through connection with others.  As we increase our knowledge, we become healthier and more resilient when approaching future problems.  It is an honor to work alongside people as they discover hidden strengths and resources. I can work with you or your family to reduce your fears, improve your mood, help cope with changes or losses in your life or help with problems in thinking. I can also assist with ways to manage work or school stress. I am non-judgemental and feel that my position is to help you to navigate, but always allow you to steer. I will work with children (3-18,) families and adults.  I have particular strength in improving parenting strategies and decreasing feelings of guilt or pain due to past hurtful experiences.

I believe healing is a collaborative effort between a client and his or her clinician.  To be most effective, a clinician must provide sincere empathy without judgement. As a Hispanic woman from New York City, I am attuned to the importance of the multicultural implications within therapy.  I assist my clients in developing clear, attainable treatment goals. Rather than adopting one methodology for all individuals and issues, I draw from a variety of methods to create a tailored approach. My clinical experience includes individual as well as group therapy  in English and Spanish.  I specialize in working with Adults diagnosed with both mental health and substance use disorder (Co-Occurring) and families affected by Intimate Partner Violence. I am dedicated to helping my clients overcome depression, anxiety, addictions to alcohol and drugs, and other stresses that affect all of us at some time in our lives and empower my client to live their best life.

My private practice generalizes in mental health and addictions/codependency treatment for men and women.   I specialize in women's mental health concerns associated with childbearing cycles, reproduction, women's mood and addictions disorders.   These issues would include pregnancy and postpartum issues, pregnancy loss, abortion issues, infertility, adoption, PMS and menopause.   I offer a comfortable, safe place to discuss what is important to you and I have a strong therapeutic background in helping you to improve your coping skills and to help achieve your goals. A healing and comfortable environment are important to me to offer my clients.   Many of my clients report that they feel comfortable telling me things that they have held secret for a long time and the relief they feel when they are able to let things go. I am also a Reiki 11 level practitioner and offer relaxation/ visualization services if this is something you feel would be helpful.   I am so appreciative to be able to offer my services and invite you to call me to set up an appointment to start your individualized journey.

I am a CT Licensed Professional Counselor and Registered Play Therapist-Supervisor that specializes in providing therapy to children and adolescents. In addition, I believe strongly in supporting caregivers.  I utilize an eclectic mix of theories and techniques to inform my practice (Client-Centered, CBT, Trauma-Focused CBT, Child-Centered Play Therapy and Mindfulness). I provide a safe space for clients to feel seen, heard, accepted, validated and supported. I believe that the therapeutic relationship heals. I also provide tools and resources to help clients learn identify, express and cope with their emotions.

Are you stressed out?  Are you having relationship problems or family conflict? Are you depressed and sad?  Come in and talk about some of the things that are impacting your life.  Start working on solutions to be the best you can be in a non-judgmental, respectful and safe place.  Throughout my career I've worked with diverse populations specializing in relationship issues, stress, anger management, trauma, depression, addictions, anxiety; and more.  Sometimes talking to a neutral person helps to start the healing process. Don't continue to be overwhelmed with your situation. Start transforming your life by locating the root of your relationship struggles, family conflict, anger, stress or sadness. Change the way you think about specific situations, find skills to manage anger, strengthen your relationships, improve your mood or work on your struggles with addiction, and more. Talking to a neutral person helps and works and it's even better in a non-judgmental and safe place!   I work with adults, families, couples, adolescents and children.

My clients are people who don't want to reinvent or change who they are, they want acceptance for being themselves.  My clients are people who wish to develop a sense of internal knowing, even if it is a quiet knowing, they are worthy, they are enough, and that they deserve acceptance.  When we feel unsupported, or judged, for who we are, it may feel safer holding back or hiding our truth.  A major component of our work together will be to build a sense of self-worth and confidence, which will allow you to feel more alive, more like your true self. My focus is working with and supporting people who come from childhoods that created the need to make themselves small or invisible in order to feel safe.  These feelings may present themselves through symptoms of trauma, anxiety, or depression. Our work will support you in understanding the way your past affects your feelings and body responses in the present.  Building internal supports and confidence, becoming who you are and choosing who you share that with takes time.  This work is about your healing.  It would be my honor to work with you and support you on this journey to finding yourself.

Shoreline Wellness Center is a behavioral health practice located in the center of West Haven. We are conveniently located one block from the West Haven green. Our team of Licensed Professional Counselors, Licensed Clinical Social Workers and Licensed Marriage and Family Therapists offer numerous mental health services for adults, children, and adolescents. Our services include: individual, group, family, marriage/couples counseling, medication management, and wellness services such as; massage therapy, yoga, meditation, and therapy dogs. In addition, we have two board certified Psychiatrists on staff, one that specializes in adult Psychiatry and one that specializes in child and adolescent Psychiatry. We also have Nurse Practitioners on staff to assist with medication management and evaluation. Our practice's main focus is on wellness and helping our clients achieve wellness for themselves both physically and mentally.  Our staff strives to deliver professional and excellent care in a kind, caring, and warm environment. We accept most commercial insurances, as well as Medicaid/Husky, Medicare, and we offer a sliding scale payment option for those without insurance. Call us today for a free, confidential, intake we are open Monday-Thursday 9AM-8PM, Fridays 9AM-6PM and Saturdays 9AM-4PM. We have evening and Saturday appointments available!

I love to support people who see themselves as a spiritual being having a human experience. You have your own unique history and have faced many challenges. Settling for less in your life is often a mirror of issues of trauma/self-worth.  I will assist you in releasing historical trauma which resulted in negative beliefs. I use techniques such as CBT, EMDR, IFS and EFT. Everyone comes gifted with their own intuitive pathways. I have clairaudient and clairsentient strengths that I use in my healing work.  I will assist you in developing your own intuition. You came to learn lessons of self-worth, to fulfill your unique purpose but more importantly to live in this creative moment. I support you in moving from powerlessness to empowerment by metabolizing life's learning, using your intuitive pathways and strengthening your connection to your Divine Self/Source.

My scope of practice includes Neurodevelopmental Disorders, Depressive Disorders, Anxiety Disorders, Trauma- and Stressor-Related Disorders, and Disruptive, Impulse-Control, and Conduct Disorders. I provide treatment primarily through Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT). I assist clients in understanding the interrelationship between thoughts, emotions, and behaviors, and how maladaptive cognitive and emotional patterns can contribute to distress and dysfunctional behavior. Through CBT, clients are supported in identifying, evaluating, and modifying unhelpful thought patterns, thereby reducing emotional distress. I am a mental health affiliate of Southington Pediatric Associates and am listed with the Early Childhood Collaborative of Southington and the Southington Behavioral Health Resource Directory. I actively collaborate with community partners and was awarded the Southington Community Well-Being Grant Initiative and have received two grants from Bread for Life. To augment my CBT work, I integrate principles from Stoic philosophy into treatment as a complementary framework for emotional regulation and resilience. Stoicism, like CBT, emphasizes cognitive appraisal, the differentiation between what is within one’s control and what is outside of one’s control, and the development of psychological flexibility.

IN-PERSON appointments in Farmington. My goal is to create a warm, safe and confidential space allowing you to discuss mental health and life problems. My ideal client is someone who is wanting to focus on improving their mental health and/or relationships. I am a caring professional who offers non-judgmental counseling support. Working with me will allow you to openly self-reflect and find positive ways forward to cope with your concerns. I help clients with mental health problems as well as clients dealing with difficult personal, home, work, relationship and caretaking concerns. I am a Licensed Professional Counselor who is caring, compassionate, honest and authentic. I use listening and client-centered skills to help you feel seen, heard and valued. I look forward to helping you begin to work on prioritizing your health and wellbeing.

Emotional pain and suffering is universal and an emotion that we can all relate to. It often produces anxiety, stress, frustration and many other emotions.  It is how we cope with it than can make a difference in how we feel, act, behave, and experience the situation. 

Often times, we fall back on maladaptive coping skills, including restriction of food intake and emotional eating.  I work with individuals suffering from mental health issues and eating disorders.  

I believe recovery is possible and with support, perspectives can shift and new discoveries can be made.  I am honored to support you on your journey to recovery. I am a trained in multiple modalities including CBT and DBT, harm reduction, strengths based approach.   I have experience working in treating both eating disorders and mental health issues and have worked in both inpatient and outpatient settings. I work from a strengths based approach and believe that a good working relationship consists of building trust. Each of us has our strengths and I am here to help you discover it. If you are struggling, please reach out. You are not alone and recovery is possible.  Together we can work towards your goals and I would be honored to be a part of your journey.

Accepting new clients for telemedicine. Starting therapy can feel scary. I create a supportive, inclusive space to explore what’s happening—and why. I build genuine relationships, meet you where you are, and help identify your strengths. I specialize in addiction and recovery, and I support clients through anxiety, depression, and life transitions. I also offer EMDR for trauma and PTSD, and I work with clients dealing with CIRS and other mold-related illnesses. Therapy can help you move forward with clarity and lasting change. I’ve heard how overwhelming it can be to choose the right provider. While many excellent therapists hold master’s degrees, those with doctoral degrees (PsyD, PhD) have more advanced training in assessment, diagnosis, and therapy. This often leads to more specialized work—helping us address not just what’s happening, but why, and how to create lasting change. I am licensed in CT, FL, and NY and hold an authorization to practice (APIT) across 40+ other states through PsyPact. Whether you're traveling for work, away at college, or relocating, we can keep your care consistent. There is no longer a need to have a disruption in your mental health care.

Throughout my career I have developed a passion for supporting children, their parents, and the family unit. I specialize in anxiety, trauma, attachment, grief, and life transitions. I currently offer support to adults whether navigating personal needs, embarking on parenthood for the first time, or seeking support managing your child’s behaviors and emotional responses. As the wife of a first-responder, I have the unique experience to aid spouses and their families as they navigate the challenges of shift work and first-responder culture, working together to manage the impact this has on marital communication and family dynamics. I believe in a collaborative approach, recognizing that while wonderful work is done within the safe space of sessions, it is when you are home putting the skills into practice where the transformative work really happens. I find Cognitive-Behavioral, Solution-Focused, and Strength-Based approaches, in combination with other modalities, to be most effective. I am here to support you and your family’s journey to living healthier and happier lives, developing effective communication skills, coping strategies, and tools to achieve the wellness you desire. Please reach out! I look forward to connecting with you and starting this journey together.

Starting therapy can feel like a big step, especially if you’ve been holding a lot on your own. My approach is grounded, collaborative, and focused on real-life change. I offer a safe and supportive, judgment-free space where you can be honest while building practical tools you can actually use day to day. Sessions balance compassion with clarity, helping you navigate overwhelm, transitions, and emotional stress at a pace that feels manageable. Therapy with me is about progress, not perfection, and moving forward with greater confidence, resilience, and steadiness.

Throughout my social work career, I have developed a passion for working with adolescents and young adults dealing with the challenges of addiction and mental health. I believe that any challenge is an opportunity for growth. My goal is to create a safe space for clients, while focusing on their strengths, to identify and achieve a life worth living. My specialization is in treating teens and young adults with substance use, depression and anxiety. I have experience and training in several types of treatment interventions. I’ve spent my career navigating treatment opportunities in the school system and mental healthcare field - speaking up for much needed change and unwavering compassion. You are not alone and I am here to help. Sometimes, the hardest thing to do is to ask for help. Be brave, ask for help - it is worth it. Please reach out and we can connect and start a journey built on trust, empathy and your inherent strength.

I am a Licensed Professional Counselor and Board-Certified Licensed Clinical Art Therapist. I completed my Masters of Arts Degree in Clinical Art Therapy and Counseling at Long Island University. I have clinical experience providing both talk and art therapy to children, adolescents, adults, and groups. I have worked in mental health facilities, medical settings, and in schools. In therapy with me, you can expect to be met with authenticity, empathy, a little humor, insight, and creativity. Together you will build on the strengths you already have to improve your relationships and solve the challenges that have brought you to therapy.  I am patient, gentle, and empathetic in my approach to therapy. I meet my clients where they are and assist them on their journey of self-discovery. In our first session together there will be an explanation of confidentiality and what you can expect from therapy. I will invite you to share what brought you to therapy and ask you some questions to explore your current situation, concerns, and goals.

At Home Outpatient Psychotherapy Environment        /                                                                                                                                        Online Therapy for Residents of Connecticut and Massachusetts Services are provided via video sessions. This setting allows for mental health and wellness to integrate into your home and daily routines. Please email to schedule a free consultation. I treat children, adults and families for a variety of life transitions and stressors, behavioral health diagnoses and symptoms (anxiety, depression, mood and trauma diagnoses and more).  Let's talk about how we can work together towards your goals.

Welcome! I am Kevin, an LCSW with over 20 years of experience working in healthcare and individual therapy.

 I specialize in helping adults navigate life’s challenges, including anxiety, depression, grief, work stress, life transitions, and caregiver issues. Whether you're dealing with a major life change, struggling to manage stress, or simply need someone to talk to, I provide a supportive, non-judgmental space where you can explore your feelings, gain insight, and build practical strategies for healing and growth. I believe in empowering clients by building on their strengths and helping them develop healthy coping skills. If you're ready to take the next step toward living a more balanced and fulfilled life, I’m here to guide you. Let’s work together to create positive change in your life.

specializations: ADHD, Adult ADHD Testing and Evaluation, Anxiety
description: Reaching out for help can feel uncomfortable—especially when you’ve been taught to handle things on your own. I specialize in working with those who are dealing with anxiety, depression, stress, and the pressure that comes with major life transitions. Whether you feel stuck, overwhelmed, disconnected, or are unsure why things feel harder than they “should,” therapy can be a place to slow down and make sense of it. My work focuses on mental health challenges, anxiety, depression, phase-of-life issues, and identity or role changes related to career, relationships, fatherhood, or aging, along with adult ADHD testing. I offer a straightforward, collaborative, and non-judgmental approach to therapy. Sessions are practical and goal-oriented while still making room for deeper emotional work. We’ll work together to understand what’s getting in the way, develop effective coping strategies and build skills that support long-term change. My goal is to create a space where you can be honest, feel understood, and work toward feeling more grounded, confident, and in control of your life. If you’re ready to take a step toward feeling better—or simply curious about whether therapy could help—I invite you to reach out.

I believe that transformation is possible for every person and that each client is a unique individual who needs to be understood from their personal worldview. I can help you identify coping skills for difficulties you are having - addiction, anxiety, trauma, anger, depression, divorce; and strategies to help you become empowered and in touch with your feelings. I can help you resolve the problems for which you are seeking treatment but also to help you to identify your strengths that will allow you to experience happiness and peace within yourself and others, and to live a life that you envision!! I have over 20 years of clinical experience and specialize in addiction, trauma, anxiety, ADHD, bipolar disorder, and depression. I conduct psychological assessments for learning disabilities/ADHD, and court ordered evaluations. My techniques include CBT, EMDR, and psychoanalysis for those interested in a more intense focus on personality change. I hold Diplomate status as a psychotherapist, and educate students in mental health/addictions counseling (over 18 years) as a Certified Clinical Supervisor. I present workshops at national conferences on addiction/mental health. I am trained in Psychoanalytic Psychotherapy for individuals seeking more intense treatment to address unconscious issues.

Relationships, friendships, transitions-and just being-can be a struggle at times. Through a holistic, multi-systemic approach, I want to help you work through these challenges.    I have more than a decade of experience, working in a hospital setting and outpatient behavioral health settings, treating depression, anxiety, adjustment, trauma, and mood disorders (my specialty). I can also assist with all stages of recovery from a Strengths Based Perspective, as well as utilizing Motivational Interviewing and Seeking Safety Skills.  With compassion and empathy, I utilize various clinical modalities including: Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, Mindfulness for Stress Reduction, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Psychodynamic Theory, and Family Systems Theory. With a team effort between the client and myself, I offer individual, couples, and family therapy services. I provide in-person sessions, but also virtual sessions.   I welcome the opportunity to work with you and be a part of your journey of change to achieve strength and inner peace.

I can help you overcome your problems. I am a highly trained and seasoned clinical psychologist practicing in Stamford, Connecticut for over 40 years. I specialize in short-term interventions. I am easy going and informal. I would be happy to discuss your problem with you and if I am not the right fit I will refer you. Please call me today. I offer specialized services in addiction relapse, anxiety, behavioral medicine, crisis intervention, depression, marriage counseling, parenting, sex therapy, sport psychology, psychological evaluations, trading psychology. I use sense of humor. I have practiced a long time. I am comfortable in all areas and problems. My sessions are instructive and geared to learning and effective change.

Being in your 20’s can feel like a whirlwind, especially as women. Some friends are getting married and having children, while others are still in school, changing careers, or partying every weekend. It can be hard to know where you fit in, and what you should be doing with your life. It's common to feel anxious, stressed, uncertain, or depressed while navigating the challenges of this time, and I understand what this exciting, yet stressful period of life can be like. I value working with young women to navigate through their unique experiences and challenges, and to bring clarity and direction to this time of life. I aim to build a collaborative relationship with clients. You are the expert on your own life, and I'm here to foster exploration, increased insight, and practical tools and action you can take to make changes in your life. Each person is different, which means I utilize tools from a variety of evidence based approaches, such as CBT, ACT, and mindfulness. I offer 100% remote therapy sessions. The best way to reach me is via email or text. Please provide your name, age, and a brief description of what your current concerns are that you'd like to address in therapy.

I'm a dedicated psychotherapist with an extensive and diverse background, spanning private practice, schools, college clinics, foster care, and hospitals, including acute care. With a passion for treating individuals of all ages, I specialize in harnessing the power of evidence-based therapies to create long-lasting positive change, focusing on the core of issues, rather than surface change. I take pride in fostering a safe, warm, and compassionate environment that embraces the unique perspectives of clients from diverse backgrounds. Even the most resistant patients find themselves engaged in the therapeutic process under my care. My approach empowers you to become an active participant in your treatment and ultimately make you your own therapist. I draw upon a rich toolkit of scientifically proven techniques, including CBT, ACT, DBT, and Exposure Therapy. These approaches are highly effective in treating anxiety, depression, trauma/PTSD, OCD, relationship issues, and low self-esteem. As a nationally certified school psychologist, I offer comprehensive psychological evaluations for learning disorders, ADHD, cognitive ability, and social-emotional functioning, with tailored treatment plans, incorporating personalized education/work strategies and interventions. I ensure that you receive detailed feedback, with an informed path to progress.

You want to move past racing thoughts and avoiding your life. Past sadness and low motivation. Past the same draining arguments with your partner. Past experiences that follow you and won't stay in the past. And past big decisions that get you stuck. You are looking to get out of feeling overwhelmed. You want to learn how to get more control. You want more than mediocre cookie-cutter therapy. You want therapy that is personalized for you and about you. You want therapy that fits into your stressful life. Even if asking for help is hard for you, you’re at a point where you are ready to do it because you want something to change. I use techniques and insight to teach you how to move past the things that are causing you discomfort, distress, and pain. I can teach you valuable approaches to solving your problems and conflicts.
