I use clinical skills & time-tested approaches that can free you up to focus on the things that matter most to you. EMDR trained by an EMDRIA-approved program. I create a completely judgement-free space for you to explore what you need to move forward. Imagine working in a relaxed and conversational way to get the support you need to break through. We focus on learning who you really are and how to best get you the help, solutions, & knowledge you need.  Are you ready to start? Call or email me now.

Human beings live within relationships. These connections are at the center of our lives, yet they can be complicated. I help adults heal past wounds that are activated in the present and improve the relationships which matter most, including the relationship with themselves. Through a collaborative approach, clients gain deep healing and an understanding of their thoughts, feelings, and behaviors, while improving communication. My approach includes evidence-based practices and strength-based skill building, such as Internal Family Systems (IFS),  Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), and Nonviolent Communication. I'm intensively trained in Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) which teaches skills to manage emotions, ways of being, and relationships through a foundation of mindfulness. Additionally, I am trained in Internal Family Systems (IFS) Level 2, which integrates with all modalities and offers deep i healing of traumas in a most compassionate way. I use evidence-based practices in a comprehensive, and personalized manner to provide quality care and healing, with warmth and compassion. The gratification of helping clients raise their self-awareness to create sustainable change, while accessing their agency, is immeasurable.

Serving clients in most US States!  Meeting you where you are... As a clinical psychologist, I understand that your life can be complex. There are various demands and expectations at different life stages that can be impacted by situational and mental health struggles. It's important to be mindful of the interaction between those demands and your stage of life. I recognize and respect that people have individual needs throughout the lifespan and I want to work closely and collaboratively with you to ensure that you reach your goals to a richer and more satisfying life. My experiences are diverse. I have worked with people across the lifespan and socioeconomic spectrum. I know how challenging asking for help can be, and I strive to work collaboratively with clients, both validating your feelings and experiences while helping you to uncover patterns and obstacles that are holding you back from reaching your goals. Feel free to visit our website at www.brownstonepsychological.com to learn more about our practice and the services we offer! If you'd like to see if your state is covered under PsyPact, please visit the website at psypact.org/mpage/psypactmap

I am a bilingual/bicultural licensed professional counselor with twenty-plus years of experience including working at a non-profit mental health clinic, a managed care company, and a public elementary school. I was born and raised in San Salvador, El Salvador, and moved to CT to pursue my master's degree in psychology. I have struggled with acculturation, parenting, and multiple losses in my life which have built my resiliency and increased my desire to help/guide others with life difficulties. Working in such diverse settings has strengthened my clinical expertise. My focus is working with children as young as 6 years old, teens, and adults. My clients are Individuals struggling with mood disorders, anxiety, ADHD, adjustment difficulties, acculturation, and grief/loss. I use an eclectic approach based on each person's needs. Call me for a free consultation and to determine if I can be of assistance. I have morning and early afternoon availability Monday through Thursday.

I am a great fit for someone who has either tried traditional talk therapy before and is looking for a more personalized and relaxed environment, or someone who has been considering starting their mental health journey for a while and is looking to get started. I specialize in treating anxiety and Obsessive Compulsive Disorder, but also find great joy in working with people who are just looking for a safe space to talk and be seen. I use a person-centered approach to help clients process trauma, build resilience, and strengthen supports for healing. In therapy, we will focus on your unique strengths and goals, creating a safe, supportive space for growth and a collaborative treatment plan. Reaching out can be the hardest part of starting something new, please feel free to text, call or email me to set up a phone consultation to see how I can helpful in supporting your goals!

specializations: Adjunctive EMDR therapy and collaboration, Anxiety, Behavioral Issues
description:
Press pause on everyday demands and focus fully on yourself with an EMDR intensive.
Intensive EMDR allows you to work at depth and address the root causes of stress, trauma, anxiety, or overwhelm—often in far fewer sessions than standard weekly therapy.

I offer half-day formats (2–6 sessions) as well as 3–5 day “life review and reset” experiences designed to create meaningful change in a short period of time. Clients often walk away with greater clarity, emotional relief, and a renewed sense of confidence and calm and can travel from any state to CT for this work together. I work with adults navigating: childhood or developmental trauma, parenting stress and burnout, divorce, separation, or relationship loss, grief and major life transitions, chronic pain and illness, panic attacks and anxiety cycles, racing thoughts, perfectionism, and people-pleasing, PTSD/ complex trauma, high performers—athletes, creatives, CEOs. This approach is ideal for motivated adults who are ready for change—whether they know trauma is driving their symptoms or simply feel stuck, off-center, or “not like themselves.” My role is to help simplify what feels overwhelming, process what has been painful, and help you move forward with clarity, balance, and a sense of control.

As a licensed therapist with extensive experience, I specialize in supporting individuals who are navigating life's complex emotional landscapes. My professional journey spans over four decades, during which I've developed expertise in addressing depression, anxiety, stress management, and career transitions.

I am particularly passionate about working with adults, helping them explore meaningful strategies for emotional wellness and personal growth. My approach is compassionate and collaborative, focusing on empowering clients to develop resilient coping mechanisms during life changes to reduce symptoms and manage issues. I work with adults using a cognitive behavioral approach. I explore with clients their beliefs, the thoughts that spin off of their beliefs as well as their behavior to collaboratively find healthy ways of thinking and behaving which in turn effects feelings. Thoughts , feelings and behavior are connected. . Life has its challenges for all of us. You don't have to face it alone. With therapeutic support, these challenges can be manageable which can help you create a life that has  joy and happiness in it.

Joey Meuser, owner and therapist at Chroma Wellness, provides compassionate, LGBTQIA+ affirming care rooted in a holistic approach that honors the mind-body connection. Specializing in CBT, ACT, and mindfulness, Joey helps clients navigate life’s challenges, break unhelpful patterns, and develop practical skills for lasting change. With warmth and understanding, Joey empowers clients to build emotional resilience, self-awareness, and a more fulfilling life. In your first session at Chroma Wellness, we’ll focus on getting to know you—your story, goals, and what brought you to therapy. We’ll discuss what’s working, what’s challenging, and what you hope to change, while answering any questions you have. It’s a collaborative, judgment-free space where you set the pace, and together we’ll create a plan that feels supportive and achievable. As a therapist, my greatest strengths are creating a warm, affirming space where clients feel truly seen, meeting people exactly where they are, and blending evidence-based techniques with a holistic, whole-person approach. I’m skilled at helping clients untangle challenges, build practical tools, and discover their own resilience.

Worries keeping you up at night? Feeling "stuck" in a career or relationship? Do you feel like you're missing out on life because of your fears? I am a licensed clinical psychologist with specialties in anxiety, behavioral sleep medicine, and health psychology. My approach to psychotherapy is practical: I hope for my clients to complete treatment with skills and tools to navigate their emotional and physical issues. All services are provided via telehealth to individuals in Connecticut and New York State. I provide evidence-based care for individuals with anxiety (panic, chronic worry, health anxiety, social anxiety), body-focused repetitive behaviors (trichotillomania, skin-picking), obsessive-compulsive and related disorders, chronic medical illness (pain, headaches, insomnia, health improvement), and services for physicians and healthcare professionals. My treatment approach incorporates acceptance and commitment therapy (ACT), mindfulness-based cognitive therapies, exposure-based treatments, cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia (CBTI), and existential philosophies. You are welcome to reach out to make sure we are a good fit.

I am a Licensed Professional Counselor Associate (LPC-A) with a background in clinical mental health and school counseling. I work with children, teens, young adults, adults, couples, and families navigating anxiety, depression, life transitions, relationship challenges, and complex trauma. Drawing from my school counseling background, I support children with emotional regulation, executive functioning, confidence, and school-related stress, and I am licensed to provide in-school clinical mental health services, including collaboration on IEP and 504 supports. My approach is holistic, collaborative, and grounded in evidence-based practice. I integrate CBT, Person-Centered, Reality, and Gestalt therapies with solution-focused, mindfulness-based, experiential, and family systems approaches—allowing therapy to remain flexible and responsive to each client’s needs. I am currently pursuing advanced training in Play Therapy (RPT track) and believe play and creativity can be healing across the lifespan. I strive to create a warm, culturally responsive space where clients feel seen, supported, and empowered to grow.

It makes sense that you may be feeling overwhelmed. The competing priorities of family/life/career can have your head spinning.If you feel like your most important relationships are suffering, reaching out to a therapist is the first step. I love to treat families as a whole, allowing for all of the different perspectives to weave together to form a much more complex picture of what is happening. However, I also believe that you can affect change in a relationship system through individual work. Historically, children's issues have been at the center of my work. Engaging with small children through play therapy, and drama therapy techniques and using art and music to connect with even the most reluctant teenager. I offer sessions in the comfort of a clients home, if attending sessions at the office is a barrier to treatment. On our free assessment phone call we determine if we are a good match and if so we schedule a session. Don't wait to feel better. Call now.

“Everyone deserves a witness to their pain”. That is something I learned years ago and have tried to live by. I spent 6.5 years in the Navy which changed my life  I am licensed in mental health and addictions, having worked in both outpatient and residential care for the past 14 years. My therapy style tends to be more eclectic rather than a one-size-fits-all approach. I have found that utilizing a strengths-based, collaborative approach works well. I am familiar with DBT, CBT, and  family systems. In this section they want me to write about what I can offer. The truth is there is not a set answer since each person is an individual...what may work for one person may not work for another. I do tend to challenge people in their thinking (in a respectful way) and encourage homework in between sessions.

I am the Director of Psychotherapy at GreenwichPsych, LLC and a Harvard- and Columbia-trained psychotherapist in Greenwich, CT. With a PhD in psychology, I specialize in evidence-based treatment for anxiety, depression, OCD, ADHD, trauma, eating disorders, body image concerns, and behavioral issues, including school avoidance. I provide therapy for adults of all ages, adolescents, and children, as well as guidance for families. Drawing on CBT, DBT, ERP, ACT, and Internal Family Systems (IFS), I create individualized treatment plans that reduce symptoms, strengthen coping, and support meaningful, lasting change. My specialty includes treating anxiety, depression, OCD, trauma, ADHD, and eating disorders. I also work with children and teens, guiding parents through Parent Management Training to improve communication and foster harmonious family interactions. In addition, I am a recognized climate-aware therapist and provide compassionate support for pet bereavement. Seeking therapy is a courageous step, and I honor the trust it takes to begin. Licensed in CT, NY, and MA, I provide in-person and telehealth care. My goal is to combine warmth, expertise, and direction as we address challenges. Together, we will build resilience, foster growth, and move toward a more meaningful and purposeful life.

I am a Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist who works with individuals and couples.  Life can be challenging at times and we can all experience stressors.  We can feel stuck in unhealthy patterns, thoughts, behaviors and/or relationships.  Therapy can be a beneficial way to explore these patterns and make healthy changes. I offer a safe and supportive space to work through these issues.  I can assist you in exploring these confusing and painful stressors.  Together we can work to identify and understand these triggers and most importantly work to move forward.  In addition, I am trained in EMDR and IFS which I have found to be very helpful in making positive changes.

Welcome! I’m a Licensed Clinical Social Worker dedicated to helping adolescents and adults navigate life’s challenges, heal from trauma, and build lasting tools for growth. I specialize in treating anxiety, depression, stress, and relationship issues. I’m PSI-trained in perinatal mood disorders and focus on body image concerns through a HAES and Intuitive Eating lens. I’m also trained in EMDR to support trauma recovery. My goal is to create a compassionate, nonjudgmental space where you feel safe, supported, and empowered to thrive. I provide a collaborative, strengths-based approach to therapy, utilizing evidence-based practices tailored to each client’s unique needs. I draw from modalities including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), Motivational Interviewing, and EMDR to foster meaningful, lasting change. I offer a complimentary 15-minute consultation to help us determine whether we’re a good therapeutic fit and to clarify your goals and expectations for therapy. Taking this first step is a powerful decision, and I look forward to supporting you on your journey.

Seeking support requires courage. If you’re feeling anxious, depressed, or lacking self-esteem, I will provide a place where you will be heard, feel validated, and hopeful. We will work on identifying and breaking down self-defeating thoughts and behaviors so you can develop the tools to gain self confidence and overcome challenges, heal, and grow. I work with adults dealing with anxiety, life changes, depression, relationship issues, chronic pain and illness, and trauma. I view therapy as a collaborative process, using humor and compassion in a non-judgmental space. I incorporate a variety of therapeutic approaches including CBT, Pain Reprocessing Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, and Emotional Awareness and Expression Therapy. Sessions can be virtual or in-person/walk-and-talk. Incorporating movement such as walking can foster an environment that supports open and honest dialogue. As a Returned Peace Corps Volunteer, mother, and person managing chronic pain, I have a deep appreciation for individuals from diverse cultures and life experiences. If you would like to learn more about my approach and think we’d be a good fit, please reach out to schedule a free consultation!

I am a clinical child psychologist, licensed in Connecticut and New York, and an Assistant Professor at the Yale Child Study Center at Westport. For nearly two decades, I’ve supported children across school and clinical settings as they face a wide range of challenges—including ADHD, executive functioning difficulties, learning disabilities, anxiety, mood disorders, social skill deficits, and other stress-related concerns. I provide evidence-based treatment and focus on helping children and adolescents further develop skills to successfully navigate challenges. I also conduct comprehensive diagnostic evaluations to better understand a child’s unique needs and guide effective treatment planning. Partnering closely with key individuals in a child's life is central to my work. As a parent myself, I understand how challenging it can be to support a struggling child. I aim to create a space where parents feel heard, validated, and empowered—working together as a team to support their child’s journey. Please feel free to reach out to learn more about my work at the Yale Child Study Center at Westport.

The same drive that pushes you to succeed can often be your greatest challenge. Whether you are a DI athlete, a first responder, a student, or a high-achieving parent, the weight of expectation is heavy. As a former DI athlete, I offer a unique perspective on the pressures of performance-driven environments. I specialize in helping you dismantle distorted thoughts and perfectionism to find mental clarity. We will partner to diminish anxiety and self-doubt, enabling you to break free from the fear of failure. In my inclusive and secure space, I empower you to overcome the barriers to your success and rediscover your best self. I use a strength-based approach that treats you as a person first and a performer second. My toolkit is tailored to your unique roadmap: I integrate EMDR to process trauma and "mental blocks," CBT and ACT to pivot away from self-sabotage, and mindfulness to help you cultivate the "flow state" and internal security necessary to thrive under pressure. I work with diverse populations—from athletes and first responders to parents and students—navigating life transitions, burnout, or grief. Whether you are looking to recover from a setback or unlock the next level of your potential, I offer weekly and intensive therapy to fit your goals. Reach out today for a consultation to start your journey.

Ready to move past the roadblocks that Depression, Anxiety, ADHD, Substance Abuse and/or OCD can create?

I specialize in helping people effectively manage and heal from the complexities of ADHD (Specialist), Mood Disorders, Substance Abuse/Gambling, Learning and relationship challenges. My approach is built on establishing a trusting, empathetic relationship so we can move directly toward your goals. We'll work together to identify and overcome internal and external obstacles so you can find greater peace and achieve your personal and professional aspirations. I'm an active, collaborative partner and dedicated to your success. I have decades of experience with using multiple gold standard treatment approaches including: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Exposure and Response Prevention, Mindfulness, Motivational Interviewing, and Relapse Prevention. I am also an Advanced ADHD Specialist (I don't just treat the symptoms, but help you build sustainable systems for success). I look forward to speaking with you and getting started on your new path forward! 
Please call or email me with any questions you might have about the treatment process.

As someone who has been in therapy myself, I know how important it is to find a therapist you can connect with. I enjoy showing my sense of humor and personality to my clients in sessions. I want to help make you feel as comfortable as possible so you can see you are in a safe place to make positive changes. I want to help you gain control back into your life and find your idea of happiness. No matter the symptoms, diagnosis or challenge, I believe in utilizing an individualized approach that is specific to helping you be your best. I completed my undergraduate studies at the University of Connecticut and my graduate degree at Capella University. Along with becoming an LPC, I have over twelve years of experience in the field. I specialize in working with teenagers and young adults, specifically women. I have expertise in areas including anxiety, depression, relationship issues and self-esteem. I also proudly serve the LGBTQIA+ community. I believe that my ability to connect with clients and build a therapeutic relationship is a key element to helping my clients.

There are times in our lives when responsibility and the stress of life seem to weigh us down. Who understands better than you? Fear and worry, sadness, life transitions and loss rob us of our joy and drain our energy. Although for some it may be somewhat intimidating, the decision to seek counseling is an important step in gaining insight and finding options. Having a trained professional listen without judgement can be a liberating experience. Finding practical ways of dealing with your concerns together with someone who understands provides a level of hope and some initial confidence. As a professional Therapist for 20+ yrs. I have practiced in NYC and then the New Fairfield-Danbury area. I have advanced training in the treatment of anxiety and depressive disorders and bereavement/loss. Another treatment focus has been marital and parent-child relationships. My professional style is casual, yet goal oriented. My approach is open, collaborative and engaged in gaining insight into the person and understanding the issues. Asking questions and actively listening to each other, we will work together in the quest of discovering insightful and practical ways to make life's journey easier to navigate.
insurance: HIP | Health Plan of New York

description: Lisa Tager is a licensed independent clinical social worker with over 30 years experience working with children, teens and adults in a variety of settings for individuals facing various life challenges. I use a strengths based approach and an eclectic model with cognitive behavioral therapy techniques, mindfulness, ACT, and self compassion. Assistance dealing with matters include anxiety, depression, grief, medical issues, low self esteem, school or work and relationship challenges. I am now accepting new telehealth clients. Most insurances and private pay accepted. Call today and lets get started!

I help individuals experiencing anxiety, life transitions, and emotional overwhelm find relief, clarity, and a stronger connection to themselves. If you are feeling stuck in worry, sadness, or self-doubt—and noticing it's impact on your relationships, motivation, or sense of direction—you don’t have to navigate it alone.

Together, we will work to reduce anxiety, depression, stress, and relationship challenges so you can move forward with greater confidence and emotional balance. Therapy with me is collaborative, supportive, and focused on helping you feel more grounded and empowered in your daily life. I specialize in the treatment of eating disorders, OCD, and perinatal mental health. I support clients in recovery through coping skills, self-esteem building, and emotional awareness. I offer a compassionate, nonjudgmental presence. By request, I use Tarot as a projective tool and integrate spiritually informed practices aligned with each client’s values. Clients choose to work with me because I offer a warm, down-to-earth approach grounded in over ten years of experience. I create a safe, supportive space where you can feel truly seen and understood while working toward meaningful change. When aligned with your values, I integrate spiritually informed practices to support deeper healing, insight, and growth.

specializations: Anger Management, Bipolar Disorder, Borderline Personality (BPD)
description: I am a licensed clinical social worker, with over 25years of experience helping others on their journey toward healing.  I have experience working with LBGTQ+, young adults, and adults.  I am well versed in dealing with clinical crises.  The treatment population that I service is supported in developing strategic ways to cope with anxiety, depression, trauma, personal development, and self-esteem. I integrate various treatment modalities to meet the goals of my clientele.  My goal is to empower clients to reach their highest level of functioning. I am trained in the following treatment modalities; Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Accelerated Resolution Therapy (ART), Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT), Brief Treatment, Empowerment Therapy, and Solution-Focused Therapy. I have a continuous desire to learn and develop therapeutic approaches to treat anxiety, depression, and trauma.  Offering an empathetic approach that allows me to meet clients exactly where they are.  I will encourage clients to stand in their truth and be willing to be a productive part of their change process.

Immediate openings for in person and tele health. Have you been feeling sad or angry? Do you worry about the smallest things but feel it prevents you from completing tasks? Are you struggling in your relationships with others? Shannon Nicholson works with people who are experiencing sadness, anxiety, and anger. She can help by guiding you with support in a non judgmental atmosphere. Through empowering you to become the best version of yourself, you will be able to gain control back in your life! Shannon Nicholson is a Licensed Clinical Social Worker who enjoys working with adolescents, adults, and families. She has worked intensively in the home with families as well as in a group home setting. Shannon's area of expertise is working with youth and adults who suffer from anxiety, depression, trauma, and conduct disorders. Her goal is to create a trusting relationship with her clients so it allows them to speak openly during sessions in order for them to meet their goals.If you are ready to start your new beginning contact Shannon for further scheduling! EMDR available!

I believe in treating your whole being - mind, body, and spirit. As both a guide and a witness to your healing, I want to give you the tools to help you see yourself, your experiences, and your emotions through a lens of self-compassion and curiosity. We will go gently and at your pace. My approach is integrative, rooted in evidence-based techniques, with mindfulness and somatic awareness weaved in. I always remain client-centered, tailoring our sessions to your unique identity and what you are experiencing and needing in the moment. Together, we will release limitations from your past, stretch your tolerance for what arises in the present, and foster hope for the future. We all carry the tools within to renew or reawaken the power to reclaim our lives. You deserve to be here, feel centered, and rise up from any disturbance which seems or appears like it's holding you back. I invite you to embrace a path toward healing.
